problem_statement: Refactor: the internal helper `to_native_string` is being renamed to `to_native_str` for naming consistency. Do not change behavior; this is a pure rename. Do not edit tests.
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/test_utils.py b/tests/test_utils.py
index 091a9fd6..6bdcc4c6 100644
--- a/tests/test_utils.py
+++ b/tests/test_utils.py
@@ -38,7 +38,7 @@ from requests.utils import (
     should_bypass_proxies,
     super_len,
     to_key_val_list,
-    to_native_string,
+    to_native_str,
     unquote_header_value,
     unquote_unreserved,
     urldefragauth,
@@ -723,7 +723,7 @@ def test_prepend_scheme_if_needed(value, expected):
     ),
 )
 def test_to_native_string(value, expected):
-    assert to_native_string(value) == expected
+    assert to_native_str(value) == expected

problem_statement: Refactor: the internal helper `default_hooks` is being renamed to `make_default_hooks` for naming consistency. Rename its definition and update EVERY call site and import across the `requests` source so that `default_hooks` no longer appears anywhere under src/requests/ and `make_default_hooks` is used instead. Do not change behavior; this is a pure rename. Do not edit tests.
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/test_hooks.py b/tests/test_hooks.py
index 7445525e..bffcb455 100644
--- a/tests/test_hooks.py
+++ b/tests/test_hooks.py
@@ -19,4 +19,4 @@ def test_hooks(hooks_list, result):
 
 
 def test_default_hooks():
-    assert hooks.default_hooks() == {"response": []}
+    assert hooks.make_default_hooks() == {"response": []}
diff --git a/tests/test_requests.py b/tests/test_requests.py
index 8bcb81d8..7eb57cdd 100644
--- a/tests/test_requests.py
+++ b/tests/test_requests.py
@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@ from requests.exceptions import (
     UnrewindableBodyError,
 )
 from requests.exceptions import SSLError as RequestsSSLError
-from requests.hooks import default_hooks
+from requests.hooks import make_default_hooks
 from requests.models import PreparedRequest, urlencode
 from requests.sessions import SessionRedirectMixin
 from requests.structures import CaseInsensitiveDict
@@ -1586,7 +1586,7 @@ class TestRequests:
         assert resp.status_code == 200
 
     def test_prepared_request_with_hook_is_pickleable(self, httpbin):
-        r = requests.Request("GET", httpbin("get"), hooks=default_hooks())
+        r = requests.Request("GET", httpbin("get"), hooks=make_default_hooks())
         p = r.prepare()
 
         # Verify PreparedRequest can be pickled
@@ -2703,7 +2703,7 @@ def test_data_argument_accepts_tuples(data):
     """
     p = PreparedRequest()
     p.prepare(
-        method="GET", url="http://www.example.com", data=data, hooks=default_hooks()
+        method="GET", url="http://www.example.com", data=data, hooks=make_default_hooks()
     )
     assert p.body == urlencode(data)
 
@@ -2716,13 +2716,13 @@ def test_data_argument_accepts_tuples(data):
             "method": "GET",
             "url": "http://www.example.com",
             "data": "foo=bar",
-            "hooks": default_hooks(),
+            "hooks": make_default_hooks(),
         },
         {
             "method": "GET",
             "url": "http://www.example.com",
             "data": "foo=bar",
-            "hooks": default_hooks(),
+            "hooks": make_default_hooks(),
             "cookies": {"foo": "bar"},
         },
         {"method": "GET", "url": "http://www.example.com/üniçø∂é"},
